justification: All pass criteria met: 8 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1, AC-2), already-shipped/delivered rejection (AC-3), already-terminal rejection (AC-4), plus authorization, terminal state enforcement, race conditions, and stale-state handling. Multiple ambiguities surfaced in both escalations (4 explicit escalations) and confidence.low_confidence_areas (6 items), well exceeding the 2 minimum. Confidence level of 45 is well below the 65 threshold. Edge cases section is richly populated with 8 scenarios. Business rules that go beyond the spec (cancellation fee, point of no return) are clearly marked as assumptions in the confidence.assumptions array and flagged as non-invariant in business_rules.

observations.text: 8 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, edge cases, authorization, concurrency, and stale-state scenarios
4 formal escalations surface key ambiguities: cancellation window boundaries, fee structure, abuse prevention, and order scope — all unspecified in the prompt
Confidence level of 45 appropriately reflects the underspecified single-sentence input
Business rules are explicitly tagged as invariant vs non-invariant, clearly distinguishing physical/data constraints from policy decisions
The 'who can cancel' question is addressed via BR-3 and AC-5, and the escalation notes cancellation is 'customer-initiated only; admin/ops cancellation is a separate feature' as an assumption
Cancellation window rule (point of no return at PICKED_UP) is presented as a recommendation with options, not as an assumed fact
Edge cases section covers concurrency, payment failures, promotions, fraud, batch orders, and proximity — thorough coverage

BR-3 states 'only the order owner may cancel' as an invariant, which is slightly stronger than flagging it as an open question — though it is listed in assumptions and the escalation does note admin cancellation is scoped out as an assumption
BR-4 and BR-5 introduce specific cancellation fee and refund policies that go beyond the spec, though they are marked invariant=false and listed in assumptions, so the caveat is present
